mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day return frequency during rate cuts drops below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals labor market deterioration that triggers immediate risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of triggered months or the t-statistic for return difference versus baseline is < 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce a positive 60-day return frequency above 58% or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above 1.0% quarter-over-quarter during a monetary tightening cycle reflects resilient supply-side capacity that supports corporate earnings, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns during tightening periods with INDPRO growth > 1.0% are not significantly higher than baseline (p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.04.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls indicate strengthening economic activity that boosts equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day return frequency during payroll increases falls below 55% or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading exceeding 2.5% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 65% of triggered months or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales, where retail sales outpace GDP, signals consumer-led momentum that drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS MoM growth exceeds GDPC1 annualized growth
failure_condition: The divergence condition fails to predict positive 20-day returns with a frequency above 60% or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant in regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production combined with rising unemployment during a post-shock recovery phase indicates fragile supply chains and weak demand, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns during this regime are positive in > 60% of instances or the signal's predictive accuracy drops below random chance in out-of-sample validation.
